reasons: Franco is an absolute-style counter when he suppresses Faramis before Nether Realm is used.
If Faramis is locked before casting ultimate, his team loses the main teamfight safety tool.

proof.summary: Faramis wants to stay alive and positioned near allies before casting Nether Realm, while Franco can suppress him and let the team burst him before that timing.
worksBestWhen: Franco catches Faramis before Nether Realm is activated.
Franco's team has immediate burst follow-up.
failureCases: Faramis casts Nether Realm before Franco reaches him.
Franco suppresses another target and leaves Faramis free.

reasons: Kaja can suppress and drag Faramis away from his team before Nether Realm.
This directly attacks Faramis's positioning requirement because Faramis needs to be near allies to maximize his ultimate.

proof.summary: Faramis wants to cast Nether Realm around grouped allies, while Kaja can suppress and pull him out of formation before the teamfight starts.
worksBestWhen: Kaja catches Faramis before ultimate activation.
failureCases: Kaja catches Faramis but the team cannot burst him.

reasons: Yin isolates Faramis from the teammates he wants to protect with Nether Realm.
If Faramis uses Nether Realm inside Yin's domain, the value is mostly self-focused instead of protecting the whole team.

proof.summary: Faramis wants to stay with allies and cast Nether Realm for a grouped teamfight, while Yin can isolate Faramis in his domain so he cannot protect teammates outside it.
worksBestWhen: Yin targets Faramis before Nether Realm is cast.
Yin has enough damage or durability to survive the domain fight.
Faramis is separated from peel or vision.
failureCases: Faramis casts Nether Realm before Yin pulls him.
Yin cannot kill or pressure Faramis inside the domain.
Faramis's team wins the outside fight while Yin is isolated.

reasons: Akai can disrupt grouped formations with Heavy Spin, reducing the value of Faramis's teamfight protection.
This is stronger than a pure anti-heal angle because Faramis is mainly a grouped teamfight protection hero, not a dedicated healer.

proof.summary: Faramis wants allies grouped inside Nether Realm, while Akai can knock enemies away, separate formations, and disrupt the teamfight area.
worksBestWhen: Akai uses Heavy Spin after Faramis's team groups for the fight.
Akai pushes priority targets away from Faramis or out of the protected area.
Akai's team follows the displacement with burst or objective control.
failureCases: Akai spins too early before Faramis commits.
Akai pushes enemies to safety instead of splitting them.
Faramis's team re-enters the fight after Heavy Spin ends.

reasons: Valentina can copy Faramis's ultimate and reduce his unique teamfight advantage.
She is more conditional than suppression or isolation counters because she must copy the correct ultimate and her team must use the copied timing well.

proof.summary: Faramis wants Nether Realm to define the teamfight, while Valentina can threaten to mirror the same kind of teamfight tool if she copies his ultimate.
worksBestWhen: Valentina safely copies Faramis before the fight.
Valentina's team coordinates around the copied ultimate timing.
failureCases: Faramis forces a fight while Valentina cannot copy or cast effectively.
